text: Agile methodology is a popular approach to managing projects, especially in software development. Instead of doing all the work at once, teams use iterative development, breaking the project into small cycles called sprints. Each sprint usually lasts two to four weeks. Before a sprint begins, the team reviews the backlog, which is a list of all tasks and features to be completed. These tasks are described as user stories, simple explanations of a feature from the user’s perspective. Every day, the team holds a short standup meeting to discuss progress and challenges. This helps everyone stay aligned and adapt quickly to changes.
